item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found myself cheering for this scip, which I think is a consequence of the Roadrunner v Coyote side of its inspirations.

Really fun, and the escalation is a bit silly but just plausible enough to not ruin it for me. Upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, basically, the car is Roadrunner and Summers is Wil E. Coyote?

Neat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How dare you! As if I'd ever be so blasé to steal an idea from someone who could actually do something about it? I'll have you know that I only steal from those who have no recourse to stop me.

I really don't, other than to write worse versions of my ideas before you can write better ones. Unfortunately you are also more disciplined and prolific. You win this time Hippo, this time.

(On a more serious note, I don't know what your unpublished story idea looks like, but this one came out of my fascination with Popcorn Sutton, Smokey and the Bandit, and the fact that I spent a good chunk of my life growing up in the Appalachians. Also, Wil E. Coyote and the Roadrunner.)

Distressingly similar: ghost Studebaker, bootleggers, mountain roads, and failed intervention, all. Inspired by a local ghost story and thinking about writing another story about the Foundation just failing.

Given this is at least the second time, I think you and I just have similar thought processes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How dare you! As if I'd ever be so blasé to steal an idea from someone who could actually do something about it? I'll have you know that I only steal from those who have no recourse to stop me.

(On a more serious note, I don't know what your unpublished story idea looks like, but this one came out of my fascination with Popcorn Sutton, Smokey and the Bandit, and the fact that I spent a good chunk of my life growing up in the Appalachians. Also, Wil E. Coyote and the Roadrunner.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, Blount Island logistics station. Not much of a base really.

No comment on your continued pattern of writing my unpublished story ideas better than I ever could, eh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oops! Fixed — th'hell did I write Florida, for?

(I think I might have gotten confused 'cuz Camp Lejeune is close to Jacksonville NC, and there's a Marine base in Jacksonville, Florida.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Camp Lejeune, Florida

North Carolina, oorah.

Also, Hippo I thought I already told you to stay out of my damn head! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did anyone else get a big Coyote and Roadrunner vibe from this article?

Emphatically upvoted. Stellar work as usual, Hippo. 😊

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a joy to read and brought a smile to my face. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for making a car into a relatable character

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like to imagine that SCP-5141 is not reanimating the dead than it did provide some drinks to Willaim Summers.

Seems like I misread something. Hmm, perhaps for all we know, SCP-5141 offered him some moonshine that led to his eventual death. But perhaps you're right, perhaps an old prohibition car dug a foe from his grave and offered him drinks.

Or poured a fuckton of moonshine down his grave and reburied him. Either way, it is hilarious to imagine what could've possibly happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was really good! It was a fun read and the ending was really good! I kind of imagine Summers and the driver sitting down and having a drink, maybe Summers got one ride in it? Anyways, great stuff!

At first, I was really surprised that nothing was blacked out… I'm still not sure how I feel about leaving it all available, but I think it could work for this specific SCP as a: "It's not bothering anyone, leave it be." But I could also see blacking out the location so that proud staff don't try to contain it… I have mixed feelings about redacting things on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember seeing this in your drafts, did not expect it to actually be published.

Reminds me of "SCP-342 - A Ticket to Ride". Except here, the stakes are not so high: if anything, part of the humor it seems comes from Agent Summers obsessive attempts to contain an anomaly that ultimately, is not that disruptive to the Veil nor dangerous and pretty insignificant in the scales of anomalies.

And the ending, it seems to be more like SCP-5141 paying tribute to a rather persistent foe.

Is the implication though that SCP-5141 can reanimate the dead so that they can drink?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wonderful article. Bizarre, tad wacky, funny and with a side of feels. Easy +1 from me.

Agent Summers has received a year-long mandatory sabbatical.

That line got me good!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agent William Summers: Nnnooo!!! You can't just let a car drive itself around on public roads. Containment means locking things up in a Site!

…

SCP-5141: haha, studebaker go vroom

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having read this, I know that my challenge thread was a success, and if we get more good writing like this, I can say it's a resounding success.

item.title: the studebaker as the roadrunner and the foundation as wile e. coyote. meep meep
item.content:encoded:
I saw fire
Fire and brimstone
Coming down
On my head

I saw fire
Fire and brimstone
Coming down
Down on me

SOURCES:

  • Image here.
  • Based on the life and times of Popcorn Sutton.
  • Submitted as part of the Imposter Syndrome Contest, intended to evoke CadaverCommander (author page here!). Notably, I'm cheating just a little — this is an idea I've had scribbled down for a long while, but on review, decided it had a very 'CadaverCommander' vibe to it.

Like the article? Click here for more of The Great Hippo's Skippos!
